claim_text: Cursor may have had a codebase-context advantage in production work because it uses local-filesystem retrieval and matched existing repository patterns on the backend refactor.
mechanism_text: A plausible mechanism is that stronger retrieval of local repository context helped Cursor align its edits to existing dependency-injection and interface-returning conventions during the backend refactor.
bounds: This mechanism is source-asserted and observational rather than experimentally isolated; the article does not separate retrieval effects from prompting, model behavior, or task-specific conditions.

claim_text: The benchmark’s final cross-tool rankings should be treated cautiously because the production comparison used different tasks and impression-based scoring.
mechanism_text: Because part of the evaluation used non-identical production tasks and was judged mainly by overall impression, the final averages compress heterogeneous evidence into precise-looking scores that cannot support a strict apples-to-apples ranking.
bounds: This explains why the ranking is weakly causal as an evaluation result, not why any one tool performed as it did on a given task.
